behaviors.description: Speech-to-text output keeps speaker labels so downstream summaries can attribute statements to attendees.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: the failure is not just local parsing; it is whether the real STT model preserves diarization/speaker labels in output. REAL-INFRA/MOCK-HIDE: a stub STT response would always include labels and hide real diarization failures. LLM-DEP: correctness depends on real model output content from STT (AI/ML), so Agentic is required. Rejected higher level (Workflow): behavior text targets one model boundary (STT label preservation), not a required multi-agentic sequence.
behaviors.test_description: Run a real audio sample with multiple speakers through the real STT API and assert returned transcript segments contain correct speaker labels consistently enough for attendee attribution checks.

behaviors.description: Transcript parsing extracts explicit task assignments (assignee + task text) into the summary-input structure.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: this behavior is a local parsing/transformation rule, not inherently a cross-module failure boundary. REAL-INFRA: no OS/network/subprocess dependency in the behavior text. LLM-DEP: none; deterministic parsing logic. Rejected higher level (Integration): text describes extraction into a structure, with no required real module handoff or external boundary.
behaviors.test_description: Feed parser fixtures containing explicit assignments (including edge punctuation/name formats) and assert exact assignee/task fields in the produced input object.

behaviors.description: Live summary includes at least one action item per attendee who received a task assignment.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: the core risk is model omission/coverage in generated summary content. MOCK-HIDE: mocked LLM outputs would trivially satisfy coverage and hide real misses. LLM-DEP: correctness depends on real LLM generation quality (who gets represented in action items). Rejected higher level (Workflow): behavior can be validated with a prepared transcript and one real summarization call; multi-agentic chaining is not explicitly required by the behavior text.
behaviors.test_description: Provide transcript(s) with known attendee-task assignments to the real summarization LLM path and assert output action_items includes at least one item for each assigned attendee.

behaviors.description: Decision-routing logic places identified decisions into the `decisions` schema section, not generic narrative fields.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: this is routing logic on already identified decisions, a local transformation boundary. REAL-INFRA: no real OS/network/subprocess need. LLM-DEP: text explicitly says 'identified decisions' (already detected), so this behavior is about placement, not model judgment quality. Rejected higher level (Integration): no explicit cross-module propagation requirement in behavior wording.
behaviors.test_description: Call routing/formatting logic with fixtures containing identified decisions and assert decisions populate only `decisions` fields while narrative fields exclude them.

behaviors.description: Key transcript decisions appear in the live summary `decisions` section rather than being buried in narrative text.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: failure appears when real model decides what counts as a key decision and where to place it. MOCK-HIDE: fixed/mock summaries hide extraction/placement quality failures. LLM-DEP: correctness directly depends on real LLM output content quality. Rejected higher level (Workflow): behavior can be tested via single real summarization step from transcript input; multi-agentic sequence is not required by wording.
behaviors.test_description: Send transcripts with clearly stated decisions through the real summary generation path and assert those decisions are present in `decisions` section, not only in freeform narrative.

behaviors.description: For audio longer than 60 minutes, service segments input, processes segments sequentially, and merges into one coherent summary.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: this is orchestration/state handoff across sequential segment-processing stages. REAL-INFRA: long-audio handling and sequencing can involve real file/processing behavior, but key risk is chained model outputs plus merge coherence. LLM-DEP + MULTI-STEP: multiple agentic steps (per-segment transcription/summarization and merged finalization) are required; correctness depends on sequence-level behavior. Rejected lower level (Agentic): behavior text explicitly requires sequential multi-step processing and merge across segments.
behaviors.test_description: Run a real >60-minute recording through the production segmentation pipeline, verify ordered per-segment processing occurred, and assert merged final summary remains structurally coherent with consolidated action items/decisions.

behaviors.description: End-to-end pipeline (upload audio, transcribe, summarize, deliver structured output) runs automatically on a real meeting recording.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: this is an end-to-end pipeline behavior where failures emerge across stage handoffs and automation boundaries. REAL-INFRA: includes real upload/input handling and external calls. LLM-DEP + MULTI-STEP: transcribe + summarize are multiple real AI/ML steps in sequence, so Workflow is the minimum boundary to catch real failures. Rejected lower levels: behavior text explicitly requires full pipeline completion on a real recording without manual intervention.
behaviors.test_description: Execute a production-like run with a real meeting audio file: upload, real transcription, real summarization, and final structured delivery; assert completion status, schema validity, and no manual recovery actions.
